highlights.description: DevX usage dashboard: a new, not-project-scoped sidebar view rendering AI-assisted development activity (sessions, prompts, commits, tool usage, cost) sourced from the OmniDevX ecosystem
Read-only, disclosure-scoped boundary — the daemon never queries the OmniDevX event store, only passes through an already-generated, already-decided-safe dashboard file

added.description: `GET /api/devx/dashboard`: passthrough handler serving `~/.plexusone/omnidevx/dashboard.json` as-is, with a 404 pointing at the generating command when the file doesn't exist yet
`DevXDashboardView` + a dependency-free renderer for the subset of dashforge's `dashboardir.Dashboard` schema devfolio's exporter emits: metric tiles, line charts with hover tooltips, and tables
`api.getDevXDashboard()`, surfacing the daemon's not-found error message (with the generating command) rather than a generic HTTP status
DevX sidebar section ("Usage Dashboard") alongside Organization — both person/machine-scoped views independent of the active tracked project

documentation.description: New guide page (guide/devx.md) explaining the not-project-scoped model and the read-only consumption boundary
Architecture docs (daemon.md, frontend.md) updated with the new handler, endpoint, and component
README: DevX Usage Dashboard feature section, daemon/component diagrams, project structure, and related projects (omnidevx-core, devfolio, dashforge)
